name: The White House
subject: President Trump's Remarks on Las Vegas
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34d0daf735e522bbadc3254745575179acd4bfe5e119402cc1831381986e137cf420c2e1ae67304910b61eeafbb42556cfebe7fcbe3cb06e

Remarks on Las Vegas
President Trump addressed the Nation on Monday morning in the wake of a tragic mass shooting on Sunday night at a music festival in Las Vegas, Nevada. The shooting claimed the lives of at least 59 victims, leaving hundreds more wounded. The President thanked the Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department and first responders for their swift and diligent efforts, and offered words of solidarity to the families impacted by the attack. “In moments of tragedy and horror, America comes together as one,” President Trump said. “We call upon the bonds that unite us -- our faith, our family, and our shared values. We call upon the bonds of citizenship, the ties of community, and the comfort of our common humanity.”

See the President’s full remarks
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34d0daf735e522bb63ec336385ff94c78b335b402b6882039709503acb47d041dfacfde70ec423098164ae6b5381a30335b70db7c2adf100
here.

Presidential Proclamation Honoring the Victims of the Tragedy in Las Vegas
Yesterday, President Trump signed a Presidential Proclamation to honor the victims of the horrible tragedy in Las Vegas, Nevada. This Proclamation orders the flag of the United States to be flown at half-staff from October 1, 2017 through October 6, 2017. "Our Nation is heartbroken. We mourn with all whose loved ones were murdered and injured in last night's horrible tragedy in Las Vegas, Nevada. As we grieve, we pray that God may provide comfort and relief to all those suffering."

Read the full Proclamation
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34d0daf735e522bbe74c99fb155fa00d9975f4f788714cee17c592846632b0ce390cd5d82e249f4788c329d75cb9b081c78bfc9731475167
here.

Hurricane Relief Update

Today, President Trump and the First Lady are in Puerto Rico to visit with individuals impacted by Hurricane Maria and receive a briefing on relief efforts.

FEMA continues to make progress through the unified efforts of the federal government, working in support of Puerto Rico’s response and recovery efforts. The full focus of the federal government is on making roads accessible and providing emergency power for the purposes of life-sustaining activities for health and medical needs, and the delivery of food and water. FEMA and its federal partners will be here for years to come, helping Puerto Rico not only in its response, but also its recovery.

Additionally, the President amended the Florida Disaster Declaration yesterday to make additional federal funding available for debris removal in the wake of Hurricane Irma.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34d0daf735e522bbbd4ffda51b9198467e9465e79e682eae21fd52497304b5d4bf0722a93b81c39a900e86050b81709c1ddce8eff9620dec
Read More.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump lead a moment of silence honoring the victims of the Las Vegas shooting | October 2, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS and VP TODAY

Today, President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump are in Puerto Rico to survey hurricane damage and relief efforts and to meet with individuals impacted by Hurricane Maria. They will also meet with Governor Kenneth Mapp of the U.S. Virgin Islands, Governor Ricardo Rossello, and senior military personnel.

Vice President Pence will travel to Phoenix, Arizona, to meet with local businesses, community leaders, and Arizona families to discuss the need for tax reform.
COMING UP

Tomorrow, the President and the First Lady will travel to Las Vegas to meet with first responders and families of the victims of the tragic shooting on Sunday night.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34d0daf735e522bb151c19d71834146884a3e3d3ceac60a082930b23775e923b5b3e91c2ff0a106b48e42d6a6de2c9c93f17af6464d8039a
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=34d0daf735e522bb1e37ca3072e173b41dd832c1dd37ca7043e394dbac5df2fd1fc8c3a9eb976697fec89e6a557c293a712cc4267f279a4a
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4ca87933586d76ff9fa05656c1c31732499eb55d8467b14d8556d4d88191e65a355d1519aa2525f974bac2044670564d4
Unsubscribe

name: Tim Kaine
subject: A note for you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
As we head into October, I wanted to write you and say thanks for everything you did in September.

Last week was a big deal -- and not just because we were facing an FEC deadline. We also faced another effort to repeal the Affordable Care Act. But thanks to you, millions of Americans can rest a little easier at night, knowing their families can access affordable health care. I often like to say that we can't have greatness without having goodness -- and to me, your activism is the goodness that makes this country great.

To be an activist, you must have faith that when people come together, we can make a real difference in the lives of those around us. Today, I thank you for your faith, and your commitment to living out our shared values. I'm proud to be on your team.

Of course, it's important to recognize the other significant effort made this month: We surpassed our very ambitious fundraising goal. That's also thanks to you, and it means a lot. It's not just about having the resources to run a smart campaign, though of course we do rely on supporters like you for that. It's also about making a statement. We're showing that we stand together, ready to win next year and keep fighting these important battles in Washington \-- like defending health care, protecting DREAMers, and advocating for a fair shot for every American, no matter what you look like or where you come from.

We have a long road ahead to Election Day 2018, and I'm going to rely on committed friends like you to support the hard work to be done between now and then. So thank you -- thank you very much for taking the time to connect with us, to pitch in what you could afford, and to speak out on the issues you care about.

Talk soon,

Tim Kaine
---
| Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Y9UboQrqCscEiT4tmIJav4Ao%2FnnjW907).
---
[](http://www.salsalabs.com/?email)

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: SCOTUS hears an important case today
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

The Republicans have successfully installed a system in states across
America that almost guarantees them a majority of legislative seats
with a minority of votes.

That system is hyperpartisan gerrymandering -- and today it’s going
before the Supreme Court in the case Gill v. Whitford, which may
ultimately decide if this illegitimate form of politicians selecting
voters is constitutional or not.

The deciding vote in this case may come down to Justice Gorsuch, who
is an illegitimate presence on the Court and could well solidify this
anti-democratic process for a generation or more. After all, how can
voters change the system if they can’t change their representatives?

That’s why we must enact a 21st Century Voting Rights Act to address
hyperpartisan gerrymandering and other abuses of our electoral system.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st_VRA_video/?t=1002&akid=311%2E356062%2EeoQYW5 ]Add your name to our petition supporting a 21st Century Voting
Rights Act today. Help solve hyperpartisan gerrymandering and other
abuses of our electoral system.

The idea of drawing district lines to benefit one party or the other
is not new; however, a confluence of specific events over the past
decade has made gerrymandering a lethal science to our democratic
legitimacy.

1. Citizens United unleashed record amounts of dark money into our
elections -- which Republicans used in 2010 to target strategic
local races that would enable them to control redistricting before
Democrats had adapted to the new reality.
2. The Shelby County v. Holder Supreme Court decision that gutted the
1965 Voting Rights Act in 2013 has allowed states like North
Carolina, Texas, and others to implement racially motivated and
hyperpartisan gerrymandering with less federal scrutiny.
3. Between 2000 and 2010, computers became so sophisticated they
could tailor-draw maps that all but guarantee which party will win
which district.

I’ve called for provisions ending hyperpartisan gerrymandering to be
included in the 21st Century Voting Rights Act, and supplemented by
state legislation if necessary. But right now, no one is demanding
this law -- except thousands of us, already standing up and speaking
as one.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st_VRA_video/?t=1003&akid=311%2E356062%2EeoQYW5 ]Join the call for reform: Add your name to the 21st Century Voting
Rights Act today.

It’s up to us to get our representatives in Congress behind this
important set of voter protections. As the Gill v. Whitford case
unfolds before the Supreme Court today, remember: there is something
you and your fellow voters can do. Your voice is valuable in this
fight.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st_VRA_video/?t=1004&akid=311%2E356062%2EeoQYW5 ]TAKE ACTION »

Authorized and paid for by LegitAction, Inc.

All content © 2017 LegitAction, Inc., All Rights Reserved

LegitAction, Inc
PO Box 620061
Middleton, WI 53562

[ https://act.legitaction.com/signup/privacy-policy/?t=1001&akid=311%2E356062%2EeoQYW5 ]Privacy Policy

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.legitaction.com/cms/unsubscribe/legitaction_unsubscribe/?akid=311%2E356062%2EeoQYW5

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: How we beat the NRA
body:
#
Team --
If you've been following our campaign for a while now, you know that we talk a lot about the epidemic of gun violence. From Las Vegas, to Miami Gardens, to here in my city of Tallahassee, there is simply too much bloodshed from guns.
And tragically, we know that the gun lobby is standing in the way of keeping our families and fellow citizens safer. I know this because they've tried to silence me, when I refused to repeal a seemingly innocuous law in Tallahassee that banned guns from our public parks. The gun lobby sued me and the other members of our city council, but fortunately, we won that fight in court. But across our state and nation, the gun lobby still holds tremendous power -- and it's time for us to step up.
Today, I'm asking you to join with us to fight back against their stranglehold on our government by sharing your personal stories of how gun violence has impacted your life, your family's lives, or your friends' lives. Too many people think it simply won't change. I am not one of those people. This fight is too important; it is literally a matter of life and death.
[**Will you share your story with us today, in hopes of changing the hearts and minds of our legislators?**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3287/9e00690/4fb0e6a9/240314df/2536379607/VEsH/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/gunviolencestories?source=em171003")
I know this is a difficult topic to discuss, especially after Sunday's tragedy. But it's important we talk about it now, and work to create change, before we lose more Americans to gun violence.
Please, remember to take care of yourselves and your loved ones -- you aren't alone in this fight.
Bring it home,
Andrew
**[SHARE YOUR STORY](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3287/9e00690/4fb0e6a9/240314df/2536379607/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/gunviolencestories?source=em171003")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3287/9e00690/4fb0e6a9/240314de/2536379607/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3287/9e00690/4fb0e6a9/240314d1/2536379607/VEsC/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3287/9e00690/4fb0e6a9/240314d0/2536379607/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer")

name: Ellen and Stephanie, EMILY's List
subject: Answering the call to help hurricane survivors
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c61a5/24c69fd9/8dcfe2ce/189d792b/2563762514/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact&source=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact")
---

Peter,

If you have been affected by Hurricanes Harvey, Irma, or Maria, the entire EMILY's List community hopes that you are safe.

But we know relief is coming far too slowly for far too many.

**Presidents Obama, Clinton, Carter, and both Bushes have asked all Americans to step up and do our part to help the survivors of these devastating hurricanes get relief and rebuild their communities.**

As a community dedicated to making a positive difference in the world, we encourage all of EMILY's List to heed that call.

**Please give whatever you can to the One America Appeal to help all of those affected -- every dollar donated goes directly to help victims:**

**[https://www.oneamericaappeal.org/#donations](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c61a5/24c69fd9/8dcfe2ce/189d7924/2563762514/VEsE/ "https://www.oneamericaappeal.org/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpswwwoneamericaappealorgdonations&utm_campaign=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact&source=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact#donations")**

Thank you for all you do every day to help communities across this country and the world,

Ellen R. Malcolm
Founder and Chair of the Board, EMILY's List

and

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

|
# EMILY's List is a community of over five million members that helps elect pro-choice Democratic women to office.
---

|
---
Paid for by EMILY's List [www.emilyslist.org](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c61a5/24c69fd9/8dcfe2ce/189d7925/2563762514/VEsF/ "http://www.emilyslist.org/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+wwwemilyslistorg&utm_campaign=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact&source=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact") and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
1800 M Street NW, Suite 375N, Washington, DC 20036 Thank you for being a supporter of EMILY's List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email [here](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c61a5/24c69fd9/8dcfe2ce/189d7926/2563762514/VEsC/ "http://action.emilyslist.org/page/s/less-email-private-bc?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact&source=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe [here](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c61a5/24c69fd9/8dcfe2ce/189d7927/2563762514/VEsD/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact&source=WQEFJ02_digital_email_20171003_HurricaneRelief_inact"). Have a question? Feel free to drop us a line at [information@emilyslist.org](mailto:information@emilyslist.org) or simply reply to this email.

Contributions or gifts to EMILY's List or endorsed candidates are not tax deductible.

name: Tom Perez
subject: Our hearts are with the people of Las Vegas.
body:
We will not give in to fear or hatred.
---
Derek --

We woke up yesterday morning to news of the worst mass shooting in modern U.S. history. Our hearts are with the people of Las Vegas, and all those affected by this despicable act of terror.

We're already hearing stories of heroism from ordinary Americans and selfless first responders who risked their lives to save others. Their bravery in this dark moment reminds us that our courage as Americans will always outlast the cruelty of cowards.

We will not give in to fear or hatred.

We are united against all those who seek to terrorize our communities. And we'll do everything we can to ensure that vicious attacks like this become a thing of the past.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

P.S. -- If you're looking for ways to help the victims of this attack, visit **[redcrossblood.org](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405234e7/4067b0a/3aafe3704/7e62990a/990019690/VEsH/ "http://www.redcrossblood.org?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+redcrossbloodorg&utm_campaign=em_20171003_tp_f1&source=em_20171003_tp_f1")** today.

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405234e7/4067b0a/3aafe3704/7e62990b/990019690/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20171003_tp_f1&source=em_20171003_tp_f1"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405234e7/4067b0a/3aafe3704/7e629908/990019690/VEsF/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171003_tp_f1&source=em_20171003_tp_f1"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405234e7/4067b0a/3aafe3704/7e629909/990019690/VEsC/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171003_tp_f1&source=em_20171003_tp_f1") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405234e7/4067b0a/3aafe3704/7e629916/990019690/VEsD/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171003_tp_f1&source=em_20171003_tp_f1"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

Our hearts are with the people of Las Vegas, and all those affected by this despicable act of terror. We're already hearing stories of heroism from ordinary Americans and selfless first responders who risked their lives to save others. Their bravery in this dark moment reminds us that our courage as Americans will always outlast the cruelty of cowards. We will not give in to fear or hatred. We are united against all those who seek to terrorize our communities. And we'll do everything we can to ensure that vicious attacks like this become a thing of the past. Tom Tom Perez Chair Democratic National Committee P.S. -- If you're looking for ways to help the victims of this attack, visit "" today. name: The Sanders Institute
subject: Civil Discourse
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friend,
"_Polarizing hostile discourse will only breed more division while, at the same time, making the real debate over issues less likely._ "
The Sanders Institute is built on three primary tenets.
We believe that a true democracy requires an informed electorate; we must consider bold, progressive solutions to the important issues that our nation faces; and as we debate our different points of view we must do so in a civil way.
James Zogby, recently elected the Chair of the Board for The Sanders Institute, wrote an opinion piece on Civil Discourse and we wanted to make sure that you had the chance to read it.
[**_The Importance of Civil Discourse_**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7af1b/bed43eb/1f86a975/75ffe19d/91323084/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/the-importance-of-civil-discourse")
The article speaks to the concerns about the harsh and angry rhetoric being used when opposing views meet--between Republicans and Democrats, the left and the right, within parties and among individuals.
[**"_Recent events have brought home just how vital it is to focus on changing the way we debate and challenging those who use personal attacks, harsh tactics, or vulgar language in an effort to stifle opposing points of view._ " --James Zogby**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7af1b/bed43eb/1f86a975/75ffe19d/91323084/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/the-importance-of-civil-discourse")
Thank you for staying engaged,
The Sanders Institute

**[DONATE»](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7af1b/bed43eb/1f86a975/75ffe19c/91323084/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sandersinstitute?refcode=em171003-full")**
PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
131 CHURCH STREET
BURLINGTON, VT 05401

The Sanders Institute is a pending 501(c)(3) organization.
131 Church Street, Burlington, VT 05401
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7af1b/bed43eb/1f86a975/75ffe19f/91323084/VEsC/ "https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe")
